assertions.title: Unmarshal then Marshal produces valid config

steps.expected: PASS - Parse->Unmarshal->Marshal->re-Parse succeeds for cluster config with nested maps, arrays, multiple value types
steps.observed: PASS - Cluster config with port, authorization block, and routes array round-trips correctly

steps.expected: PASS - Simple config with listen address and authorization timeout round-trips
steps.observed: PASS - listen=127.0.0.1:4222 and authorization.timeout=0.5 preserved through round-trip

steps.expected: PASS - All scalar types (string, int, float, bool, duration, time) round-trip correctly
steps.observed: PASS - All 6 scalar types round-trip with value equality confirmed field-by-field

steps.expected: PASS - Array of map structs (users list) round-trips
steps.observed: PASS - 2 users with user/password fields survive Parse->Unmarshal->Marshal->re-Parse

assertions.title: v1 config parsed by v2 then marshaled is v1-parseable

steps.expected: PASS - All v1 sample configs survive v1 parse -> v2 parse -> v2 marshal -> v1 re-parse pipeline with equivalent values
steps.observed: PASS - All 10 subtests pass: simple_top_level, booleans, nested_map_with_arrays, cluster_config, single-quoted_strings, array_of_maps, datetime_and_bool, bcrypt_password, convenient_numbers, JSON-style_nested_blocks

steps.action: Verify test exercises v1/v2 equivalence check
steps.expected: Test uses conf.Parse (v1) and v2.Parse, verifies reflect.DeepEqual, then marshals and re-parses with v1
steps.observed: Test confirms v1 and v2 produce identical maps via reflect.DeepEqual, marshals via helper, re-parses with v1 conf.Parse, and compares values key-by-key

assertions.title: Full pipeline — file to struct to modified struct to file

steps.expected: PASS - ParseFile->Unmarshal->modify Port=4223->Marshal->write->ParseFile->Unmarshal->verify Port=4223 and all other fields unchanged
steps.observed: PASS - Realistic config with host, port, debug, max_payload, ping_interval, cluster (name/port/routes), authorization (user/password/timeout) all verified field-by-field after Port modification

steps.expected: PASS - Multiple field modifications (Port, Debug, MaxConn) preserved while Host unchanged
steps.observed: PASS - Port=4223, Debug=true, MaxConn=2000 all modified and verified; Host=0.0.0.0 unchanged

steps.expected: PASS - Marshal(nil) returns error without panic
PASS - Marshal((*Config)(nil)) returns error without panic
steps.observed: PASS - Marshal of nil pointer returns non-nil error

steps.expected: PASS - Marshal(struct{}{}) returns valid (possibly empty) config
steps.observed: PASS - Empty struct produces valid output (empty or parseable)

steps.expected: PASS - All key-value lines in Marshal output use ': ' separator consistently
steps.observed: PASS - Test marshals struct with name/port/debug fields, scans all output lines, confirms every key-value line contains ': ' separator

steps.action: Verify test covers the assertion requirement (deterministic, documented separator)
steps.expected: Test checks every non-structural line for ': ' separator
steps.observed: Test iterates all lines, skips empty/brace lines, asserts strings.Contains(line, ": ") on each

assertions.title: AST round-trip vs struct round-trip fidelity

steps.expected: PASS - AST round-trip (ParseASTRaw->Emit) preserves all 8 comments from input
steps.observed: PASS - All 8 comments (# and //) found in AST output; output is valid parseable config

steps.expected: PASS - Struct round-trip (Unmarshal->Marshal) does not include any comments
steps.observed: PASS - No comments found in struct output; output is valid parseable config

steps.expected: PASS - AST preserves comments, struct does not; both produce valid parseable output with equivalent resolved values
steps.observed: PASS - All 6 comments present in AST output, absent in struct output; both parse to equivalent maps; original map DeepEqual to AST map

steps.expected: PASS - AST preserves $base_port reference; struct resolves to integer 4222
steps.observed: PASS - AST output contains '$base_port' and comment; struct output contains neither; both valid config

steps.expected: PASS - AST preserves include directive; struct does not contain include
steps.observed: PASS - AST output contains 'include' and 'users.conf'; struct output has neither; struct output valid config

steps.expected: PASS - AST preserves original separator styles (=, :, whitespace); struct normalizes all to ': '
steps.observed: PASS - AST output preserves '=' for port; struct output uses ': ' for all lines; both valid config

summary: Tested 6 assertions (VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-006): all 6 passed. Total of 16 individual test functions executed across cross_test.go and marshal_test.go, all PASS. The cross-area integration tests comprehensively cover: unmarshal-marshal round-trips with various types, v1-v2-v1 compatibility across 10 config samples, full file-based modify pipelines, nil/empty error handling, key separator consistency, and the fundamental AST vs struct round-trip fidelity distinction.
